author: @RandomAxeOfKindness
text: I remain convinced that the #1 reason for gigantic wind turbines is that only large corporations can afford them.  No homeowner is going to install one of those monsters, so they're a good way to keep wind power in the hands of existing utilities.

author: @zanzabarter
text: The question is how many birds will they kill?

replies.author: @Chris-go2iz
replies.text: Numbers vary wildly from different studies, but in 2013 and 2014 estimates were anywhere from 140K to 679K per year, which is alot, until you look at studies showing that house cats kill anywhere between 988 Mil and 4 Billion per year. If you consider that amount of habitat that gets destroyed for fossil fuel projects, the number of birds killed is negligible. That doesn't even account for the impact of Climate Change which is much harder to calculate.

author: @EduardQualls
text:
"Quite dangerous to the local wildlife" ???  As of 2019, in the US, annually, *wind turbines cause 573k bird deaths, while cars kill 200 million, buildings kill 600 million and **_cats kill 2.4 billion birds._*  So, your information/fable is not backed by data.
[Source: US News & World Report; Dec. 24, 2019; by Cecelia Smith-Schoenwalder]

author: @dennissorensen8765
text:
On topic, I was recently told that golf balls have their little "holes" in order to improve their aerodynamic properties! 🤨
It was a race car tuner, who told me this, as they also incorporate this concept into the inner surfaces on engine air intakes in order to obtain faster airflow over the inner surfaces of the intake system. 🤯

author: @halloen33
text: All good but storing energy is our biggest problem. Thanks for your great content 👍

replies.author: @ua420
replies.text:
It was an issue in 2020 but not in 2024 or 2030. With EVs pushing Lithium batteries down you just can buy batteries at $100 per kWh now. And if you use refurbished batteries from teslas it goes well below $50 per kWh.

There's no typical energy usage profile world-wide. But many households around the globe cover daily energy needs with  just 1-5 kWh of energy.

replies.author: @halloen33
replies.text: @ua420   Well, if we take all the energy storage there is and it only lasts 1 hour. Problem and lithium and kobolt are not cheap enough. We do need rethinking energy.
